executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict has escalated significantly with 653 incidents in 24h. Iran has launched widespread ballistic missile attacks across the region targeting Israel, UAE (Dubai Airport, Abu Dhabi port), and Gulf states, while the IRGC claims to have targeted the USS Abraham Lincoln with 4 missiles. The death of Supreme Leader Khamenei has triggered succession planning with a Temporary Leadership Council now operational. US military confirmed 3 troops killed in Operation Epic Fury — the first American combat casualties. Israel has mobilized 100,000 reservists and dropped 2,000 bombs on Iran.
whats_new: Death toll from US-Israeli airstrike on Iranian girls school rises to 165 (from 148 previously)
Israel calls up 100,000 reservists and reinforces eastern border with rapid response units
Maersk suspends all vessel crossings in Strait of Hormuz due to conflict
US releases footage of B-2 stealth bombers en route to attack Iranian ballistic missile facilities
UK announces review of terror threat level amid Iran strikes
Trump claims 48 Iranian officials killed, states new Iranian leadership asked to negotiate
confirmed_events: Confirmed by multiple sources: 3 US service members killed, 5 wounded in direct engagement with Iranian-backed forces — first US combat casualties
Confirmed: Iranian ballistic missiles hit Dubai Airport and Sharjah, causing fires
Confirmed: Iranian attack on Abu Dhabi kills 3, injures 58; UAE air defense intercepted 20 ballistic missiles, 2 cruise missiles, 311 drones
Confirmed: 9 killed in Iranian missile strike on Beit Shemesh, highest single-day Israeli casualty toll
Confirmed: Kuwait reports 1 fatality, 32 injuries from Iranian attacks at Al Adan Hospital
Confirmed: US CENTCOM sank Iranian Jamaran-class corvette in Chabahar Port
unverified_emerging: IRGC claims 560 US personnel killed and injured in Bahrain missile strike (source: RIA Novosti, unverified, US has not confirmed)
Reports of explosion in Tehran — nature unclear, whether airstrike or air defense (source: Islamic Resistance Iraq TG channel)
Massive fire at US military base in Qatar following Iranian missile and drone attacks (source: Hezbollah Electronic Army TG, unverified by US)

people_travel: Over 1,000 flights by major Middle Eastern airlines stranded or diverted due to airspace closures in Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Doha
Key transit hubs Dubai, Abu Dhabi and Doha closed airspace
supply_chain: Maersk suspends vessel crossings in Strait of Hormuz — critical global oil chokepoint carrying 20% of world oil
Strait of Hormuz closure risk threatens global oil supply chains
market_macro: Brent crude jumped 10% to approximately $80/barrel, could spike to $100 if Strait closes
Israeli hospitals overcrowded with wounded patients indicating significant casualties

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The conflict is at a critical inflection point. Iran has demonstrated willingness to strike deep into Israeli territory and target US allies across the Gulf, while the US continues major combat operations with B-2 bombers and carrier assets. The death of Khamenei creates a power vacuum but IRGC remains operational and has signaled continued escalation capacity. Diplomatic signals from Trump (new Iranian leadership wants to negotiate) suggest potential off-ramp, but military momentum continues. Expect continued Iranian proxy attacks on US bases in Iraq and Gulf states.
escalation_risks: IRGC claims targeting USS Abraham Lincoln — if confirmed as direct hit, could trigger US retaliation against Iranian territory at unprecedented scale
Strait of Hormuz closure would catastrophic global oil supply disruption
Iranian attacks on Gulf state infrastructure could draw Saudi Arabia and other Gulf states into direct conflict
Russia or China intervention to support Iran could expand conflict scope
Mass casualty attacks on Israeli population centers could trigger even more devastating Israeli response
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: Trump's statement that new Iranian leadership asked to negotiate and he agreed provides a potential diplomatic opening. However, IRGC military leadership remains intact and has pledged continued confrontation. Any de-escalation would require either: (1) Iran accepting a negotiated ceasefire while saving face, or (2) one side achieving decisive military advantage. The Temporary Leadership Council succession process could either accelerate negotiations or harden Iranian resolve.
